title: Command Lifecycle, Main Boundary, and Decomposition
description: A one-file script reads global arguments during import, mixes file access with counting and printing, cannot be tested without exiting, and changes behavior when imported. Learners cumulatively produce testable command lifecycle and function map through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.
Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.
Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.
